justification: All pass criteria met: (1) All three input sources represented — Bird's domain rules, MJ's architecture, and Pippen's operational notes are explicitly referenced throughout. (2) All 3 ACs present with exact values ($4.50, $5.00, $0.01). (3) Pippen's operational notes explicitly included — JSDoc is a 'hard requirement', high-traffic context mentioned (500 req/sec), unit tests only confirmed. (4) $0.01 floor labeled as a 'domain invariant' in terminology_alignment. (5) Quantity boundary >= 10 preserved exactly in domain_rules and acceptance criteria.

observations.text: JSDoc requirement explicitly marked as 'Pippen hard requirement for on-call operability' — not optional
All 3 ACs preserved with exact numeric values including AC3 ($0.01 floor)
$0.01 floor explicitly called out as 'domain invariant' in terminology_alignment section
Quantity boundary >= 10 preserved exactly with testable assertion showing boundary (9 vs 10)
Terminology alignment section adds useful domain-to-code mapping beyond minimum requirements
Low confidence area about floating-point rounding is a genuinely useful flag for Shaq
Assumptions section transparently surfaces unstated constraints (negative prices, fractional quantities)
